gnss-product-management API

YAML-driven GNSS product discovery, query expansion, and dependency resolution.

Client

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Client — public API layer for GNSS product search and download.

class gnss_product_management.client.FoundResource(*, product: str, source: str, uri: str, parameters: dict[str, str]=<factory>, date: datetime | None = None, local_path: Path | CloudPath | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A discovered product resource — either a local file or a remote URI.

property center: str

Analysis center identifier (e.g. 'WUM'), or '' if not applicable.

date: datetime.datetime | None
property directory: str

Parent directory of the resource file.

property downloaded: bool

True if the file has been downloaded and exists on disk.

property filename: str

Filename (basename) of the resource.

property hostname: str

Server hostname, or '' for local resources.

property is_local: bool

True if this resource was found on the local filesystem.

local_path: AnyPath | None
model_config = {'arbitrary_types_allowed': True}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

model_post_init(context: Any, /) → None

This function is meant to behave like a BaseModel method to initialise private attributes.

It takes context as an argument since that’s what pydantic-core passes when calling it.

Parameters:

  • self – The BaseModel instance.

  • context – The context.

parameters: dict[str, str]
property path: Path | None

Return the local Path if this is a local resource, else None.

product: str
property protocol: str

Transport protocol (e.g. 'ftp', 'https', 'file').

property quality: str

Solution quality/type (e.g. 'FIN', 'RAP'), or '' if not applicable.

source: str
uri: str
class gnss_product_management.client.GNSSClient(product_registry: ProductRegistry, workspace: WorkSpace, *, max_connections: int = 4)[source]

Bases: object

High-level client for searching and downloading GNSS products.

GNSSClient is the single entry point for all product retrieval. It wraps ProductQuery, DownloadPipeline, and ResolvePipeline as internal implementation details.

Use from_defaults() to get started with the bundled specs:

# Search only — no local sink needed
client = GNSSClient.from_defaults()
results = client.search(date, product="ORBIT")

# Search and download
client = GNSSClient.from_defaults(base_dir="/data/gnss")
results = client.search(date, product="CLOCK", parameters={"TTT": "FIN"})
paths = client.download(results, sink_id="local")

# Full dependency resolution
client = GNSSClient.from_defaults(base_dir="/data/gnss")
resolution, lockfile = client.resolve_dependencies("spec.yaml", date, sink_id="local")
Parameters:

  • product_registry – Configured ProductRegistry with loaded product and center specs.

  • workspace – WorkSpace with registered local directories.

  • max_connections – Maximum concurrent connections per host.

display() → None[source]

Display the client’s loaded product registry and workspace specs.

download(results: list[FoundResource], *, sink_id: str) → list[Path][source]

Download product candidates to the local sink.

Pass a pre-sliced list to limit how many files are fetched, e.g. client.download(results[:1], sink_id="local").

Parameters:

  • results – Ranked FoundResource list from search() or ProductQuery.search(). Each result must carry a date (set automatically by the query builders).

  • sink_id – Local resource identifier (alias registered in the workspace, e.g. "local").

Returns:

Paths to successfully downloaded (and decompressed) files.

classmethod from_defaults(base_dir: Path | str | None = None, *, local_alias: str = 'local', max_connections: int = 4) → GNSSClient[source]

Construct a client from the bundled default specs.

Loads the pre-built DefaultProductEnvironment and creates a fresh WorkSpace from the bundled local layout specs. If base_dir is provided, all local specs are registered against that directory, enabling downloads and local-first resolution.

Parameters:

  • base_dir – Root directory for local product storage. If None, the client operates in search-only mode (no local sink).

  • local_alias – Alias for the registered local resource (default "local"). Used as the sink_id in download() and resolve_dependencies().

  • max_connections – Maximum concurrent connections per host.

Returns:

A configured GNSSClient instance.

query() → ProductQuery[source]

Return a fluent ProductQuery builder.

This is the preferred entry point for building searches. Chain calls to narrow the query, then call ProductQuery.search() or ProductQuery.download() to execute:

results = (
    client.query()
    .for_product("ORBIT")
    .on(date)
    .where(TTT="FIN")
    .sources("COD", "ESA")
    .search()
)
Returns:

A ProductQuery bound to this client.

resolve_dependencies(dep_spec: DependencySpec | Path | str, date: datetime, *, sink_id: str) → tuple[DependencyResolution, Path | CloudPath | None][source]

Resolve all dependencies in a spec for the given date.

Accepts a DependencySpec object or a path to a YAML file. Checks local disk first, then downloads missing files. Returns immediately if a lockfile already exists for (package, task, date, version).

Parameters:

  • dep_spec – Dependency specification — a DependencySpec instance, or a path (str or Path) to a YAML file.

  • date – Target date (timezone-aware datetime).

  • sink_id – Local resource identifier for storing resolved files.

Returns:

A (DependencyResolution, lockfile_path) tuple.

search(date: datetime, product: str | dict, *, parameters: dict | None = None, preferences: list[SearchPreference] | None = None, local_resources: list[str] | None = None, remote_resources: list[str] | None = None) → list[FoundResource][source]

Search for products matching the given criteria.

Delegates to ProductQuery internally: builds queries, lists remote directories, expands matches, infers parameters, and ranks by preference and protocol.

Parameters:

  • date – Target date (timezone-aware datetime).

  • product – Product name as a string (e.g. "ORBIT") or a dict with name, and optionally version / variant.

  • parameters – Parameter constraints, e.g. {"TTT": "FIN"}.

  • preferences – Optional preference cascade for ranking results. Each SearchPreference specifies a parameter and an ordered list of preferred values.

  • local_resources – Restrict to these local resource IDs.

  • remote_resources – Restrict to these remote center IDs.

Returns:

Ranked list of FoundResource objects, best first. Local/file results precede remote ones; within each protocol tier results are ordered by preferences.

class gnss_product_management.client.ProductQuery(wormhole: WormHole, search_planner: SearchPlanner)[source]

Bases: object

Fluent builder for constructing and executing a GNSS product search.

Constructed via GNSSClient.query() — do not instantiate directly.

Chain method calls to build the query, then call search() or download() to execute:

results = (
    client.query("ORBIT")
    .on(date)
    .where(TTT="FIN")
    .sources("COD", "ESA")
    .prefer(TTT=["FIN", "RAP", "ULT"])
    .search()
)

paths = (
    client.query("CLOCK")
    .on(date)
    .where(TTT="FIN")
    .sources("local", "COD")
    .download(sink_id="local")
)
Parameters:

  • fetcher – WormHole used for directory listing and file download.

  • query_factory – SearchPlanner used to build SearchTarget objects from product specs.

  • product – Product name (e.g. "ORBIT") or dict with name, and optionally version / variant.

download(sink_id: str, *, limit: int | None = None) → list[Path][source]

Search and download results in one call.

Parameters:

  • sink_id – Local resource alias to download into (e.g. "local").

  • limit – Maximum number of files to download. None downloads all results.

Returns:

Paths to successfully downloaded files.

Raises:

ValueError – If for_product() or on() have not been called.

for_product(product: str | dict) → ProductQuery[source]

Set the target product for the query.

Parameters:

product – Product name (e.g. "ORBIT") or dict with name, and optionally version / variant.

Returns:

self for chaining.

on(date: datetime) → ProductQuery[source]

Set the target date for the query.

Parameters:

date – Timezone-aware datetime.

Returns:

self for chaining.

on_range(start: datetime, end: datetime, *, step: timedelta = datetime.timedelta(days=1)) → ProductQuery[source]

Set a date range for the query.

Searches are run for every date from start to end (inclusive) with the given step (default: 1 day). Results from all dates are merged into a single flat list from search().

Parameters:

  • start – First date to query (inclusive).

  • end – Last date to query (inclusive).

  • step – Interval between consecutive dates (default: 1 day).

Returns:

self for chaining.

Raises:

ValueError – If start is after end.

prefer(**kwargs) → ProductQuery[source]

Define a preference cascade for ranking results.

Keyword arguments map parameter names to an ordered list of preferred values. The first entry is most preferred:

.prefer(TTT=["FIN", "RAP", "ULT"])

Multiple calls accumulate preferences in call order.

Parameters:

**kwargs – Parameter name → ordered list of preferred values.

Returns:

self for chaining.

search() → list[FoundResource][source]

Execute the query and return ranked results.

Returns:

Ranked list of FoundResource objects, best first. Local/file results precede remote ones; within each protocol tier results are ordered by preferences. When on_range() was used, results from all dates are returned as a flat combined list.

Raises:

ValueError – If neither on() nor on_range() has been called.

sources(*ids: str) → ProductQuery[source]

Restrict the search to specific local or remote sources.

Pass any mix of registered local aliases and remote center IDs. Each ID is resolved at search() time — local aliases are routed to local disk, everything else is treated as a remote center ID.

Calling sources() with no arguments is an error; omit the call entirely to search all available sources.

Parameters:

*ids – One or more source identifiers.

Returns:

self for chaining.

Raises:

ValueError – If no IDs are provided.

where(**parameters) → ProductQuery[source]

Constrain product parameters.

Keyword arguments map parameter names to required values, e.g. where(TTT="FIN") or where(TTT=["FIN", "RAP"]).

Parameters:

**parameters – Parameter name → value (or list of values).

Returns:

self for chaining.

Environments

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Environments — setup and configuration for product resolution.

Provides the two core configuration objects:

  • ProductRegistry — loads YAML specs and builds the full catalog chain (parameters → formats → products → remote search planner).

  • WorkSpace — registers local storage directories and maps them to LocalResourceSpec layouts.

class gnss_product_management.environments.ProductRegistry[source]

Bases: object

Unified container for the specification / factory layer.

Incrementally loads YAML specs via add_*() methods, then calls build() to derive the full catalog chain:

ParameterCatalog → FormatCatalog → ProductCatalog

Remote resource specs loaded via add_resource_spec() are built into ResourceCatalog objects that support source_product() and sink_product() for remote query resolution.

After building, classify() parses a product filename into structured metadata (product name, format, version, variant, parameters).

_parameter_catalog

Built parameter catalog (available after build()).

_format_catalog

Built format catalog (available after build()).

_product_catalog

Built product catalog (available after build()).

_catalogs

Built remote resource catalogs (available after build()).

__init__() → None[source]

Initialise an empty registry with no specs loaded.

add_format_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a format specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_parameter_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a parameter specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_product_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a product specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_resource_spec(path: Path | str) → None[source]

Load and register a remote resource specification YAML file.

Parameters:

path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

property all_queries: list[SearchTarget]

Flattened list of every search target across all remote centers.

build() → None[source]

Build the full catalog chain from loaded specs.

Must be called after all add_*() methods. Builds: ParameterCatalog → FormatCatalog → ProductCatalog → remote ResourceCatalog objects.

property catalogs: list[ResourceCatalog]

All registered remote resource catalogs.

property centers: list[str]

Alias for resource_ids.

classify(filename: str, parameters: list[Parameter] | None = None) → dict[str, str] | None[source]

Parse a product filename and return its metadata.

Parameters:

  • filename – A product filename, optionally including a directory path and/or compression extension.

  • parameters – Optional hard constraints. Products whose fixed parameters conflict with a supplied value are skipped.

Returns:

A dict with keys product, format, version, variant, and parameters on match, or None if no product template matches.

display() → None[source]

Print a rich summary of loaded products and registered remote centers.

Prints two tables to the terminal:

  • Products — every product name with its versions and variants.

  • Remote Centers — every registered data center with its available products, protocols, and hostnames.

Requires the rich package (bundled as a project dependency).

get(center_id: str) → ResourceCatalog[source]

Retrieve a remote resource catalog by center identifier.

Parameters:

center_id – Data center identifier.

Returns:

The matching ResourceCatalog.

static match_pinned_query(found: Product, incoming: Product) → Product | None[source]

Check if a found query matches an incoming product based on pinned parameters.

Parameters:

  • found – Product from the resource catalog.

  • incoming – Product being searched for.

Returns:

The incoming product with matched values filled in, or None if pinned parameters conflict.

property resource_ids: list[str]

Identifiers for all registered remote resource centers.

sink_product(product: Product, resource_id: str, date: datetime) → SearchTarget[source]

Resolve the remote directory/filename for uploading product.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to upload.

  • resource_id – Remote resource identifier.

  • date – Target date for computed fields.

Returns:

A SearchTarget with resolved paths.

Raises:

KeyError – If no matching entry exists.

source_product(product: Product, resource_id: str) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Resolve a product into all matching SearchTargets for a remote resource.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to resolve.

  • resource_id – Remote resource identifier.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects.

Raises:

KeyError – If resource_id or product.name is not found.

class gnss_product_management.environments.RegisteredLocalResource(*, name: str, base_dir: str, spec: LocalResourceSpec, item_to_dir: dict[str, str], server: Server)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A local resource spec that has been bound to a base directory.

base_dir is stored as a URI string so that it can represent both local paths (/data/gnss) and cloud locations (s3://bucket/prefix). Use the base_path property to obtain the appropriate Path or CloudPath object for filesystem operations.

name

Human-readable identifier for this resource.

Type:

str

base_dir

Base directory URI (local path or cloud URI).

Type:

str

spec

The underlying local resource specification.

Type:

LocalResourceSpec

item_to_dir

Mapping of item names to their subdirectory.

Type:

dict[str, str]

server

A file-protocol Server wrapping base_dir.

Type:

Server

base_dir: str
property base_path: Path | CloudPath

The base directory as a Path or cloud path.

item_to_dir: dict[str, str]
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str
server: Server
spec: LocalResourceSpec
class gnss_product_management.environments.WorkSpace[source]

Bases: object

Registry of local storage directories and their layout specifications.

Manages the mapping between LocalResourceSpec definitions (loaded from YAML) and concrete base directories on disk or in cloud storage. Each registered spec gets a Server(protocol='file') so that local resources can be queried with the same ResourceQuery interface used for remote servers.

Also provides product-resolution methods (source_product, sink_product, lockfile_dir, find_local_files) once bound to a ProductRegistry via bind().

_registered_specs

Mapping of spec names to registered resources.

_alias_map

Mapping of aliases to canonical spec names.

_resource_specs

Loaded but not-yet-registered spec objects.

_product_catalog

Product catalog (available after bind()).

_parameter_catalog

Parameter catalog (available after bind()).

Usage:

ws = WorkSpace()
ws.add_resource_spec('local_config.yaml')
ws.register_spec(base_dir='/data/gnss', spec_ids=['local_config'], alias='local')
__init__()[source]

Initialise an empty workspace with no specs loaded.

add_resource_spec(path: Path | str, id: str | None = None) → None[source]

Load a LocalResourceSpec from a YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Path to the YAML specification file.

  • id – Optional override for the spec name. Defaults to the name declared inside the YAML file.

Raises:

AssertionError – If path does not exist or a spec with the same name is already registered.

bind(product_registry: ProductRegistry) → None[source]

Inject catalog references from a built ProductRegistry.

Must be called before using source_product(), sink_product(), or find_local_files().

Parameters:

product_registry – A fully built registry with catalogs.

display() → None[source]

Print a rich summary of loaded specs and registered local resources.

Prints two tables:

  • Loaded Specs — every spec loaded via add_resource_spec(), with its name and product list.

  • Registered Resources — every resource bound to a base directory via register_spec(), with its alias(es), base directory, and the products it covers.

Requires the rich package (bundled as a project dependency).

find_local_files(query: SearchTarget, date: date | None = None) → list[Path | CloudPath][source]

Search local or cloud storage for files matching a query.

Works identically for local Path and cloud CloudPath base directories.

Parameters:

  • query – SearchTarget with directory and filename patterns.

  • date – Optional date for interpolating computed fields.

Returns:

Sorted list of matching paths (local or cloud).

Raises:

KeyError – If the query’s server is not registered.

lockfile_dir(resource_id: str) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Return the dependency lockfile directory for a resource.

For local resources this is a subdirectory of the base directory. For cloud resources (e.g. s3://bucket/prefix) it is an equivalent cloud path, enabling distributed workers to share lockfile state via cloud storage.

Parameters:

resource_id – Local resource identifier.

Returns:

Path to the lockfile directory (created if needed).

register(spec: LocalResourceSpec | Path | str, base_dir: Path | str, alias: str | None = None) → None[source]

Register a local resource specification in one step.

Convenience alternative to add_resource_spec() + register_spec(). Accepts a LocalResourceSpec object or a path to a YAML file.

Parameters:

  • spec – A LocalResourceSpec, or a path to a YAML file.

  • base_dir – Root directory on disk for the resource.

  • alias – Optional alias that also maps to this resource.

Raises:

ValueError – If base_dir overlaps with an existing registration or alias is already taken.

register_spec(base_dir: Path | CloudPath | str, spec_ids: list[str], alias: str | None = None) → None[source]

Bind loaded spec(s) to a base directory and register the result.

base_dir may be a local filesystem path or a cloud URI such as s3://bucket/prefix. When multiple spec_ids are given they are merged into a single LocalResourceSpec.

Parameters:

  • base_dir – Root directory for the resource (local path or cloud URI).

  • spec_ids – One or more previously loaded spec identifiers.

  • alias – Optional alias that also maps to this resource.

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If base_dir does not exist or any spec_id has not been loaded.

  • ValueError – If alias is already in use or base_dir overlaps with an existing registration.

property resource_ids: list[str]

Identifiers for all registered local resources.

sink_product(product: Product, resource_id: str, date: date) → SearchTarget[source]

Resolve the local directory for a product on a given date.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to locate.

  • resource_id – Local resource identifier.

  • date – Target date for computed template fields.

Returns:

A SearchTarget with resolved directory path.

Raises:

KeyError – If resource_id or product.name is not found.

source_product(product: Product, resource_id: str) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Resolve a product into SearchTarget objects for a local resource.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to resolve.

  • resource_id – Local resource identifier.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects.

Raises:

KeyError – If resource_id or product.name is not found.

gnss_product_management.environments.paths_overlap(p1: Path | CloudPath | str, p2: Path | CloudPath | str) → bool[source]

Check whether two paths share a common ancestor-descendant relationship.

For local paths, checks the resolved filesystem hierarchy. For cloud URIs, falls back to string prefix comparison (cloud paths have no symlinks to resolve).

Parameters:

  • p1 – First path or URI.

  • p2 – Second path or URI.

Returns:

True if either path is a parent of (or equal to) the other.

Factories

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Factories — Layer 2: query construction, resource discovery, and file fetching.

class gnss_product_management.factories.DependencyLockFile(*, date: str, package: str, task: str, version: str, requires_date: bool = True, timestamp: str = <factory>, products: list[LockProduct] = <factory>, metadata: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Top-level lockfile: a fully-resolved, reproducible product manifest.

The lockfile is date-scoped — one lockfile per processing day. Identity key: (task, date, version) where version is the gnss-product-management package version.

date: str
metadata: dict | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

package: str
products: list[LockProduct]
requires_date: bool
task: str
timestamp: str
version: str
class gnss_product_management.factories.DownloadPipeline(env: ProductRegistry, workspace: WorkSpace, *, transport: WormHole | None = None, max_connections: int = 4)[source]

Bases: object

Download FoundResource objects to the local workspace.

Already-local resources return immediately with their existing path. Remote resources are downloaded via WormHole.

A per-file sidecar <filename>_lock.json is written alongside every successfully resolved file if one does not already exist.

Parameters:

  • env – The product registry with built catalogs.

  • workspace – Workspace with registered local resources.

  • max_connections – Maximum concurrent connections per host.

  • transport – Optional shared WormHole instance.

run(resources: FoundResource | list[FoundResource], date: datetime, *, sink_id: str = 'local_config') → Path | None | list[Path | None][source]

Download found resources to the workspace.

Parameters:

  • resources – A single FoundResource or a list of them.

  • date – Target date for computing sink directory.

  • sink_id – Local resource alias to download into.

Returns:

A Path (or None on failure) for a single resource, or a list of paths for multiple resources.

class gnss_product_management.factories.LockfileWriter(lockfile_dir: Path, *, package: str = 'PRIDE')[source]

Bases: object

Write a DependencyResolution to a JSON lockfile.

Parameters:

  • lockfile_dir – Directory where lockfiles are stored.

  • package – Package name (e.g. 'PRIDE').

write(resolution: DependencyResolution, date: datetime, *, version: str | None = None) → Path[source]

Persist resolution as a lockfile.

Parameters:

  • resolution – The completed dependency resolution.

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Package version; defaults to the installed version.

Returns:

Path to the written lockfile.

class gnss_product_management.factories.ProductRegistry[source]

Bases: object

Unified container for the specification / factory layer.

Incrementally loads YAML specs via add_*() methods, then calls build() to derive the full catalog chain:

ParameterCatalog → FormatCatalog → ProductCatalog

Remote resource specs loaded via add_resource_spec() are built into ResourceCatalog objects that support source_product() and sink_product() for remote query resolution.

After building, classify() parses a product filename into structured metadata (product name, format, version, variant, parameters).

_parameter_catalog

Built parameter catalog (available after build()).

_format_catalog

Built format catalog (available after build()).

_product_catalog

Built product catalog (available after build()).

_catalogs

Built remote resource catalogs (available after build()).

__init__() → None[source]

Initialise an empty registry with no specs loaded.

add_format_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a format specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_parameter_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a parameter specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_product_spec(path: Path | str, id: str = 'default') → None[source]

Load and register a product specification YAML file.

Parameters:

  • path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

  • id – Unique identifier for this spec (default 'default').

add_resource_spec(path: Path | str) → None[source]

Load and register a remote resource specification YAML file.

Parameters:

path – Filesystem path to the YAML file.

property all_queries: list[SearchTarget]

Flattened list of every search target across all remote centers.

build() → None[source]

Build the full catalog chain from loaded specs.

Must be called after all add_*() methods. Builds: ParameterCatalog → FormatCatalog → ProductCatalog → remote ResourceCatalog objects.

property catalogs: list[ResourceCatalog]

All registered remote resource catalogs.

property centers: list[str]

Alias for resource_ids.

classify(filename: str, parameters: list[Parameter] | None = None) → dict[str, str] | None[source]

Parse a product filename and return its metadata.

Parameters:

  • filename – A product filename, optionally including a directory path and/or compression extension.

  • parameters – Optional hard constraints. Products whose fixed parameters conflict with a supplied value are skipped.

Returns:

A dict with keys product, format, version, variant, and parameters on match, or None if no product template matches.

display() → None[source]

Print a rich summary of loaded products and registered remote centers.

Prints two tables to the terminal:

  • Products — every product name with its versions and variants.

  • Remote Centers — every registered data center with its available products, protocols, and hostnames.

Requires the rich package (bundled as a project dependency).

get(center_id: str) → ResourceCatalog[source]

Retrieve a remote resource catalog by center identifier.

Parameters:

center_id – Data center identifier.

Returns:

The matching ResourceCatalog.

static match_pinned_query(found: Product, incoming: Product) → Product | None[source]

Check if a found query matches an incoming product based on pinned parameters.

Parameters:

  • found – Product from the resource catalog.

  • incoming – Product being searched for.

Returns:

The incoming product with matched values filled in, or None if pinned parameters conflict.

property resource_ids: list[str]

Identifiers for all registered remote resource centers.

sink_product(product: Product, resource_id: str, date: datetime) → SearchTarget[source]

Resolve the remote directory/filename for uploading product.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to upload.

  • resource_id – Remote resource identifier.

  • date – Target date for computed fields.

Returns:

A SearchTarget with resolved paths.

Raises:

KeyError – If no matching entry exists.

source_product(product: Product, resource_id: str) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Resolve a product into all matching SearchTargets for a remote resource.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to resolve.

  • resource_id – Remote resource identifier.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects.

Raises:

KeyError – If resource_id or product.name is not found.

class gnss_product_management.factories.ResolvePipeline(env: ProductRegistry, workspace: WorkSpace, *, max_connections: int = 4, transport: WormHole | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Find → Download → Lockfile for every dependency in a spec.

Uses ProductQuery, DownloadPipeline, and LockfileWriter internally. All dependencies are resolved in parallel via a ThreadPoolExecutor.

Fast path: if an aggregate lockfile already exists for the (package, task, date, version) identity, returns immediately without searching or downloading.

Parameters:

  • env – The product registry with built catalogs.

  • workspace – Workspace with registered local resources.

  • max_connections – Maximum concurrent connections per host.

  • transport – Optional shared WormHole instance. If provided, the pipeline reuses it instead of creating a new one — useful when GNSSClient already holds a pool.

run(spec: DependencySpec, date: datetime, *, sink_id: str = 'local_config', centers: list[str] | None = None, download: bool = True) → tuple[DependencyResolution, Path | CloudPath | None][source]

Resolve all dependencies in spec for date.

Parameters:

  • spec – The dependency specification.

  • date – Target date (timezone-aware datetime).

  • sink_id – Local resource alias for download destination and lockfile storage.

  • centers – Restrict remote search to these center IDs.

  • download – If True (default), download remote resources.

Returns:

A tuple of (DependencyResolution, lockfile path or None if nothing was resolved).

class gnss_product_management.factories.SearchPlanner(product_registry: ProductRegistry, workspace: WorkSpace)[source]

Bases: object

Lazy search planner — narrows parameter ranges, resolves on demand.

Uses ProductRegistry for remote resource catalogs, WorkSpace for local resource resolution, ProductCatalog (nested version→variant→Product hierarchy), and ParameterCatalog for fallback regex patterns and computed date-field resolution.

_env

The product registry backing this planner.

_workspace

Workspace with registered local resources.

Usage:

sp = SearchPlanner(
    product_registry=registry,
    workspace=workspace,
)

results = sp.get(
    datetime.date(2024, 1, 1),
    product={"name": "ORBIT"},
    parameters={"AAA": ["WUM", "COD"]},
)
__init__(product_registry: ProductRegistry, workspace: WorkSpace)[source]

Initialise the search planner.

Parameters:

  • product_registry – Built ProductRegistry with catalogs and remote resource catalogs ready.

  • workspace – WorkSpace with registered local resources.

static build_queries_from_planner(templates: list[Product], date: datetime, query_planner: WorkSpace | ProductRegistry, resource_selection: list[str] | None = None) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Build search queries from a given planner and resource selection.

Parameters:

  • templates – List of product templates to build queries from.

  • date – Target date for resolving computed directory fields.

  • query_planner – A WorkSpace (local) or ProductRegistry (remote) with resource_ids and source_product interface.

  • resource_selection – Optional list of resource IDs to restrict to.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects built from the planner.

get(date: datetime, product: dict[str, str | list[str]], parameters: dict[str, str | list[str]] | None = None, local_resources: list[str] | None = None, remote_resources: list[str] | None = None) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Narrow parameter ranges and return searchable resources.

Parameters:

  • date – Target date for computed metadata fields.

  • product – Product query dict with name, optionally version and variant.

  • parameters – User constraints on metadata fields. Unset fields remain as wildcard regex patterns.

  • local_resources – If given, restrict to these local collection IDs.

  • remote_resources – If given, restrict to these remote center IDs.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects.

Raises:

ValueError – If the product, version, or variant is not found.

class gnss_product_management.factories.SearchPreference(*, parameter: str, sorting: list[str] = <factory>, description: str = '')[source]

Bases: BaseModel

One slot in the preference cascade.

description: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

parameter: str
sorting: list[str]
class gnss_product_management.factories.SourcePlanner(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Protocol

Shared query-side interface for search planners.

Both RemoteSearchPlanner and LocalSearchPlanner satisfy this protocol, allowing SearchPlanner and other consumers to treat local and remote resources uniformly.

Registration (register()) is intentionally not part of this protocol — each planner accepts different spec types at setup time.

register(spec, **args) → None[source]

Register a resource specification with the planner.

Parameters:

  • spec – The resource specification to register.

  • **args – Additional keyword arguments.

property resource_ids: list[str]

Return identifiers for all registered resources.

sink_product(product: Product, resource_id: str, **args) → SearchTarget[source]

Return the target path for product on resource_id.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to resolve.

  • resource_id – Identifier of the target resource.

  • **args – Additional keyword arguments.

Returns:

A SearchTarget with resolved path templates.

source_product(product: Product, resource_id: str, **args) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Resolve product against the resource identified by resource_id.

Parameters:

  • product – Product to resolve.

  • resource_id – Identifier of the target resource.

  • **args – Additional keyword arguments.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects.

class gnss_product_management.factories.WorkSpace[source]

Bases: object

Registry of local storage directories and their layout specifications.

Manages the mapping between LocalResourceSpec definitions (loaded from YAML) and concrete base directories on disk or in cloud storage. Each registered spec gets a Server(protocol='file') so that local resources can be queried with the same ResourceQuery interface used for remote servers.

Also provides product-resolution methods (source_product, sink_product, lockfile_dir, find_local_files) once bound to a ProductRegistry via bind().

_registered_specs

Mapping of spec names to registered resources.

Type:

dict[str, gnss_product_management.environments.workspace.RegisteredLocalResource]

_alias_map

Mapping of aliases to canonical spec names.

Type:

dict[str, str]

_resource_specs

Loaded but not-yet-registered spec objects.

Type:

dict[str, gnss_product_management.specifications.local.local.LocalResourceSpec]

_product_catalog

Product catalog (available after bind()).

Type:

gnss_product_management.specifications.products.catalog.ProductCatalog | None

_parameter_catalog

Parameter catalog (available after bind()).

Type:

gnss_product_management.specifications.parameters.parameter.ParameterCatalog | None

Usage:

ws = WorkSpace()
ws.add_resource_spec('local_config.yaml')
ws.register_spec(base_dir='/data/gnss', spec_ids=['local_config'], alias='local')
__init__()[source]

Initialise an empty workspace with no specs loaded.

  • id – Optional override for the spec name. Defaults to the name declared inside the YAML file.

Must be called before using source_product(), sink_product(), or find_local_files().

product_registry – A fully built registry with catalogs.

Print a rich summary of loaded specs and registered local resources.

Prints two tables:

  • Loaded Specs — every spec loaded via add_resource_spec(), with its name and product list.

  • Registered Resources — every resource bound to a base directory via register_spec(), with its alias(es), base directory, and the products it covers.

Requires the rich package (bundled as a project dependency).

Search local or cloud storage for files matching a query.

Works identically for local Path and cloud CloudPath base directories.

Return the dependency lockfile directory for a resource.

For local resources this is a subdirectory of the base directory. For cloud resources (e.g. s3://bucket/prefix) it is an equivalent cloud path, enabling distributed workers to share lockfile state via cloud storage.

Bind loaded spec(s) to a base directory and register the result.

base_dir may be a local filesystem path or a cloud URI such as s3://bucket/prefix. When multiple spec_ids are given they are merged into a single LocalResourceSpec.

class gnss_product_management.factories.WormHole(*, max_connections: int = 4, product_registry=None)[source]

Bases: object

Search for files described by SearchTarget objects.

For each target, lists the remote (FTP/HTTP) or local directory, matches product.filename.pattern against the listing, and populates directory.value and filename.value on the target.

_connection_pool_factory

Factory managing per-host connection pools.

_env

Optional product registry for parameter back-filling.

Usage:

targets = sp.get(date=..., product=..., parameters=...)
transport = WormHole()
results = transport.search(targets)

for st in results:
    print(st.server.hostname, st.product.filename.value)
__init__(*, max_connections: int = 4, product_registry=None) → None[source]

Initialise the transport.

Parameters:

  • max_connections – Maximum connections per host pool.

  • product_registry – Optional ProductRegistry for parameter back-filling after a filename match.

download_one(query: SearchTarget, local_resource_id: str, local_factory: WorkSpace, date: datetime) → Path | CloudPath | None[source]

Synchronously download matched files for one search target.

Skips the download if the destination file already exists and is non-empty.

Parameters:

  • query – The resolved search target with filename value.

  • local_resource_id – Target local resource identifier.

  • local_factory – Planner for resolving local sink paths.

  • date – Target date for computing sink directory.

Returns:

Path (local or cloud) to the downloaded file, or None on failure.

search(targets: list[SearchTarget]) → list[SearchTarget][source]

Search every target’s server/directory for matching files.

Targets are grouped by (hostname, directory) so each unique remote directory is listed exactly once. Pattern matching for every target in the group runs against the shared listing. The filename.value field is set on each returned target.

Parameters:

targets – SearchTarget objects to search.

Returns:

A list of SearchTarget objects with filename.value already populated — one per matched filename.

Specifications

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Specifications — Layer 1: Pydantic models and resolution logic for GNSS product specs.

Subpackages

  • parameters — Parameter, ParameterCatalog

  • format — FormatFieldDef, FormatVersionSpec, FormatSpec,

    FormatCatalog, FormatSpecCatalog

  • products — Product, PathTemplate, ProductCatalog

  • local — LocalCollection, LocalResourceSpec

  • remote — Server, ResourceSpec, SearchTarget,

    ResourceCatalog

  • dependencies — DependencySpec, ResolvedDependency

Lockfile models (LockProduct, DependencyLockFile) live in gnss_product_management.lockfile but are re-exported here for convenience.

class gnss_product_management.specifications.Catalog[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Abstract base for built catalogs.

Subclasses must implement:

@classmethod
def build(cls, ...) -> Self:
    ...

The build classmethod is the only way to construct a catalog in production code. Direct __init__ is still available for testing or deserialization.

abstractmethod classmethod build(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) → Catalog[source]

Build a concrete catalog instance from lower-layer specs.

Parameters:

  • *args – Positional arguments specific to each subclass.

  • **kwargs – Keyword arguments specific to each subclass.

Returns:

A fully built catalog instance.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gnss_product_management.specifications.Dependency(*, spec: str, required: bool = True, description: str = '', constraints: dict[str, str]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A single product dependency.

constraints: dict[str, str]
description: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

required: bool
spec: str
class gnss_product_management.specifications.DependencyLockFile(*, date: str, package: str, task: str, version: str, requires_date: bool = True, timestamp: str = <factory>, products: list[LockProduct] = <factory>, metadata: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Top-level lockfile: a fully-resolved, reproducible product manifest.

The lockfile is date-scoped — one lockfile per processing day. Identity key: (task, date, version) where version is the gnss-product-management package version.

date: str
metadata: dict | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

package: str
products: list[LockProduct]
requires_date: bool
task: str
timestamp: str
version: str
class gnss_product_management.specifications.DependencyResolution(spec_name: str, resolved: list[ResolvedDependency] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: object

Aggregated resolution result for all dependencies in a spec.

spec_name

Name of the dependency specification.

Type:

str

resolved

List of ResolvedDependency results.

Type:

list[gnss_product_management.specifications.dependencies.dependencies.ResolvedDependency]

property all_required_fulfilled: bool

True if every required dependency has been resolved.

property fulfilled: list[ResolvedDependency]

Dependencies that have been resolved (not missing).

property missing: list[ResolvedDependency]

Dependencies that could not be resolved.

product_paths() → dict[str, str][source]

Return a {spec: uri} mapping for resolved local files.

Values are URI strings that work for both local paths and cloud locations. Pass them through gnss_product_management.utilities.paths.as_path() to get a path object suitable for filesystem operations.

Returns:

Dict mapping spec names to their local-path or cloud URIs.

resolved: list[ResolvedDependency]
spec_name: str
summary() → str[source]

Return a one-line summary of resolution counts.

Returns:

Human-readable summary string.

table() → str[source]

Return a formatted table of all resolved dependencies.

Returns:

Multi-line string with columns for spec, required, status, and path.

class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatRegistry(*, formats: dict[str, ~gnss_product_management.specifications.format.spec.FormatSpec]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Read-only registry of reusable format definitions.

Built from a FormatSpecCollection by resolving every metadata field’s default value against a ParameterCatalog.

formats

Mapping of format name to resolved FormatSpec.

Type:

dict[str, FormatSpec]

classmethod build(format_spec: FormatSpecCollection, metadata_catalog: ParameterCatalog) → FormatRegistry[source]

Build a FormatRegistry by resolving metadata field defaults.

Verifies that every metadata field referenced in a format version has either a pattern value or an entry in metadata_catalog.

Parameters:

  • format_spec – Raw format specification collection.

  • metadata_catalog – Global parameter catalog for field defaults.

Returns:

A FormatRegistry with all fields resolved.

Raises:

  • AssertionError – If a field is missing both a pattern and a catalog entry.

  • ValueError – If a file template placeholder has no corresponding metadata field.

formats: dict[str, FormatSpec]
get_format(name: str) → FormatSpec[source]

Retrieve a format by name.

Parameters:

name – Format name.

Returns:

The matching FormatSpec.

Raises:

KeyError – If name is not registered.

get_version(format_name: str, version: str) → FormatVersionSpec[source]

Retrieve a specific version of a format.

Parameters:

  • format_name – Format name.

  • version – Version identifier.

Returns:

The matching FormatVersionSpec.

Raises:

KeyError – If the format or version is not found.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatSpec(*, description: str = '', versions: dict[str, ~gnss_product_management.specifications.format.spec.FormatVersionSpec]=<factory>, compression: list[str] = <factory>)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A top-level format definition (e.g. RINEX, PRODUCT, TABLE).

Contains the format description and a mapping of version → FormatVersionSpec. Each version in turn maps variant names to filename templates and metadata field definitions.

Note

This is the format-library model. For the product-facing model that binds a specific format+version+variant to parameter lists and filename templates, see FormatVariantSpec.

compression: list[str]
description: str
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

versions: dict[str, FormatVersionSpec]
class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatSpecCatalog(*, formats: dict[str, VersionCatalog[FormatVariantSpec]])[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Collection of raw format-variant specifications loaded from YAML.

formats: dict[str, VersionCatalog[FormatVariantSpec]]
classmethod from_yaml(path: Path) → FormatSpecCatalog[source]

Load from a YAML file containing pre-built format specs.

Expected YAML layout:

FORMAT_NAME:
  name: FORMAT_NAME
  versions:
    "1":
      name: "1"
      variants:
        variant_name:
          name: FORMAT_NAME
          version: "1"
          variant: variant_name
          parameters:
            - name: PARAM
          filename: "{PARAM}..."
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatSpecCollection(*, formats: dict[str, ~gnss_product_management.specifications.format.spec.FormatSpec]=<factory>)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Collection of format specifications from the formats: YAML key.

formats: dict[str, FormatSpec]
classmethod from_yaml(path: str | Path) → FormatSpecCollection[source]

Load from a YAML file.

Accepts two layouts:

  1. Wrapped — a top-level formats: key whose value is a mapping of format name → FormatSpec-compatible dict.

  2. Flat (format_spec.yaml convention) — format names are top-level keys; each entry has versions → variants → {parameters, filename} which are converted to the metadata / file_templates expected by FormatVersionSpec.

Parameters:

path – Path to the YAML file.

Returns:

A FormatSpecCollection instance.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatVariantSpec(*, name: str, version: str | None = None, variant: str | None = None, parameters: list[dict] | None = <factory>, filename: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A single file-format variant binding: name × version × variant → parameters + filename.

This is a resolved “leaf” entry in the format spec YAML — it names which format (e.g. RINEX), which version ("3"), which variant (observation), and lists the parameters and filename template that together define a concrete file shape.

filename: str | None
materialize(parameter_catalog: ParameterCatalog) → Product[source]

Materialize against a ParameterCatalog to produce a Product.

Parameters:

parameter_catalog – Global parameter catalog for defaults.

Returns:

A Product with resolved parameters.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str
parameters: list[dict] | None
variant: str | None
version: str | None
class gnss_product_management.specifications.FormatVersionSpec(*, description: str | None = None, notes: str | None = None, metadata: dict[str, ~gnss_product_management.specifications.format.spec.FormatFieldDef | None]=<factory>, file_templates: dict[str, str]=<factory>, compression: list[str] = <factory>, **extra_data: Any)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A single version of a format (e.g. RINEX v3, PRODUCT v1).

compression: list[str]
description: str | None
file_templates: dict[str, str]
metadata: dict[str, FormatFieldDef | None]
model_config = {'extra': 'allow'}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

notes: str | None
class gnss_product_management.specifications.LocalCollection(*, directory: str, description: str | None = None, items: list = <factory>)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A group of product specs sharing a directory template.

description: str | None
directory: str
items: list
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

class gnss_product_management.specifications.LocalResourceSpec(*, name: str = 'default', description: str | None = None, collections: dict[str, ~gnss_product_management.specifications.local.local.LocalCollection]=<factory>, source_file: Path | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Root model for a local storage layout.

A single spec maps collection names to LocalCollection objects. Multiple specs can be merged via merge() so that different YAML files (e.g. per-project or per-workflow) combine into one unified layout.

collections: dict[str, LocalCollection]
description: str | None
classmethod from_yaml(path: str | Path) → LocalResourceSpec[source]

Load from a YAML file.

Accepts either a top-level local: wrapper or a flat file whose top-level key is collections:.

Parameters:

path – Path to the YAML file.

Returns:

A LocalResourceSpec instance.

classmethod merge(specs: Sequence[LocalResourceSpec]) → LocalResourceSpec[source]

Merge multiple local storage specs into one.

Later specs override collections with the same name. Items within identically-named collections are combined (union).

Parameters:

specs – Sequence of specs to merge.

Returns:

A new LocalResourceSpec with combined collections.

model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

name: str
source_file: Path | None

class gnss_product_management.specifications.ParameterCatalog(parameters: list[Parameter])[source]

Bases: object

Registry of parameters with pattern defaults and computed-field support.

Replaces MetadataCatalog. Compatible with register_computed_fields().

parameters

Mapping of parameter names to Parameter objects.

computed(name: str, pattern: str | None = None, *, description: str | None = None)[source]

Decorator that registers a computed parameter field.

Parameters:

  • name – Parameter name.

  • pattern – Regex pattern for the parameter value.

  • description – Human-readable description.

Returns:

A decorator that wraps the compute function.

defaults() → dict[str, str][source]

Return {name: pattern} for every parameter with a pattern.

Returns:

Mapping of parameter names to their regex patterns.

classmethod from_yaml(yaml_path: str | Path) → ParameterCatalog[source]

Load parameter definitions from a meta-spec YAML file.

Does not register computed fields — call register_computed_fields() separately after loading.

get(name: str, default=None) → Parameter | None[source]

Retrieve a parameter by name.

Parameters:

  • name – Parameter name.

  • default – Value returned when name is not found.

Returns:

The Parameter or default.

interpolate(template: str, date: datetime, *, computed_only: bool = False) → str[source]

Substitute {NAME} placeholders in template.

Parameters:

  • template – String containing {NAME}-style placeholders.

  • date – Reference datetime for computed fields.

  • computed_only – If True, only replace computed parameters.

Returns:

The interpolated string.

merge(other: ParameterCatalog) → ParameterCatalog[source]

Merge another catalog into this one.

Duplicate names are overwritten by other with a warning.

Parameters:

other – Catalog to merge.

Returns:

A new ParameterCatalog with combined parameters.

register(name: str, pattern: str | None = None, *, compute: Callable[[datetime], str] | None = None, description: str | None = None) → Parameter[source]

Register or update a parameter, optionally adding a compute function.

Parameters:

  • name – Parameter name.

  • pattern – Regex pattern for the parameter value.

  • compute – Callable that derives the value from a datetime.

  • description – Human-readable description.

Returns:

The newly created or updated Parameter.

resolve_params(params: list[Any], date: datetime) → Any[source]

Set .value on computed parameters from date.

Parameters:

  • params – List of Parameter-like objects.

  • date – Reference datetime for computed fields.

Returns:

The same params list with computed values filled in.

class gnss_product_management.specifications.PathTemplate(*, pattern: str, value: str | None = None, description: str | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

A template pattern with {NAME}-style placeholders, resolved via derive().

derive(parameters: list[Parameter]) → None[source]

Replace {PARAM} placeholders in pattern with parameter values.

Parameters:

parameters – List of parameters to substitute.

description: str | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

pattern: str
to_regex(parameter_catalog: ParameterCatalog) → str[source]

Convert the template pattern into a regex with named capture groups.

Each {PARAM} placeholder is replaced with (?P<PARAM>pattern) using the parameter’s regex from parameter_catalog. Literal characters outside placeholders are escaped.

Parameters:

parameter_catalog – Catalog supplying regex patterns for each parameter name.

Returns:

A regex string suitable for re.fullmatch().

value: str | None

Utilities

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Utilities — domain helper functions for GNSS product metadata.

class gnss_product_management.utilities.IGSAntexReferenceFrameType(*values)[source]

Bases: Enum

IGS ANTEX reference frame identifiers.

Maps each IGS reference frame to its canonical lowercase string used in ANTEX filenames (e.g. igs20.atx).

IGS05 = 'igs05'
IGS08 = 'igs08'
IGS14 = 'igs14'
IGS20 = 'igs20'
IGSR3 = 'igsR3'
gnss_product_management.utilities.as_path(uri: str | Path | CloudPath) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Return a Path or CloudPath.

Detects the scheme from uri and dispatches accordingly:

  • s3://, gs://, az:// → CloudPath

  • anything else (including bare POSIX paths) → Path

Parameters:

uri – A URI string, local path string, Path, or CloudPath.

Returns:

The appropriate path object for the given URI.

Examples:

>>> as_path("/data/gnss")
PosixPath('/data/gnss')
>>> as_path("s3://my-bucket/gnss-data")
S3Path('s3://my-bucket/gnss-data')
>>> as_path(Path("/data/gnss"))
PosixPath('/data/gnss')
gnss_product_management.utilities.register_computed_fields(registry) → None[source]

Register all date-derived computed fields onto registry.

Iterates over the built-in _COMPUTED_FIELDS list and calls registry.computed() for each one, wiring the pure date transformation functions into the catalog.

Parameters:

registry – A ParameterCatalog instance to extend.

Lockfile

Author: Franklyn Dunbar

Lockfile — models and I/O for reproducible product manifests.

This package provides Pydantic models and file-system operations for creating, reading, writing, and validating lockfiles. A lockfile records the exact resolved products (with hashes, sizes, and source URLs) for a single processing date, enabling reproducible GNSS PPP runs.

Public API

Models:

LockProduct, LockProductAlternative, DependencyLockFile

Manager:

LockfileManager

Operations:

validate_lock_product(), build_lock_product(), get_lock_product_path(), get_lock_product(), write_lock_product(), get_dependency_lockfile_name(), get_dependency_lockfile(), write_dependency_lockfile(), get_package_version()

Enums:

HashMismatchMode

class gnss_product_management.lockfile.DependencyLockFile(*, date: str, package: str, task: str, version: str, requires_date: bool = True, timestamp: str = <factory>, products: list[LockProduct] = <factory>, metadata: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: BaseModel

Top-level lockfile: a fully-resolved, reproducible product manifest.

The lockfile is date-scoped — one lockfile per processing day. Identity key: (task, date, version) where version is the gnss-product-management package version.

date: str
metadata: dict | None
model_config = {}

Configuration for the model, should be a dictionary conforming to [ConfigDict][pydantic.config.ConfigDict].

package: str
products: list[LockProduct]
requires_date: bool
task: str
timestamp: str
version: str
class gnss_product_management.lockfile.HashMismatchMode(*values)[source]

Bases: Enum

How to handle hash mismatches during lockfile validation.

STRICT = 'strict'
WARN = 'warn'

Bases: BaseModel

A single resolved product entry in the lockfile.

alternatives: list[LockProductAlternative]

An alternative (mirror / fallback) source for a locked product.

class gnss_product_management.lockfile.LockfileManager(lockfile_dir: Path | CloudPath | str)[source]

Bases: object

Facade for dependency lockfile lifecycle.

_dir

Directory where aggregate lockfiles are stored.

Parameters:

lockfile_dir – Directory for aggregate lockfile storage. May be a local Path, a URI string, or a CloudPath (e.g. s3://bucket/locks).

__init__(lockfile_dir: Path | CloudPath | str) → None[source]

Initialise the manager.

Parameters:

lockfile_dir – Directory for aggregate lockfile storage.

build_aggregate(products: list[LockProduct], package: str, task: str, date: datetime | str, version: str | None = None) → DependencyLockFile[source]

Build a DependencyLockFile from per-file sidecar products.

Parameters:

  • products – Lock products collected from sidecar files.

  • package – Package name (e.g. 'PRIDE').

  • task – Task name (e.g. 'PPP').

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Package version; defaults to installed version.

Returns:

A new DependencyLockFile ready to be saved.

exists(package: str, task: str, date: datetime, version: str | None = None) → bool[source]

Check if a lockfile exists for the given identity.

Parameters:

  • package – Package name.

  • task – Task name.

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Optional package version.

export_lockfile(package: str, task: str, date: datetime, version: str | None = None) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Return the path to the aggregate lockfile for sharing.

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If no lockfile exists for the identity.

import_lockfile(path: Path | CloudPath | str, strict: bool = False) → DependencyLockFile[source]

Import a lockfile from another machine or cloud location.

Validates each product’s hash. In warn mode (default), mismatches are logged but products are kept. In strict mode, invalid products are removed so the caller can re-resolve them.

Parameters:

  • path – Path or URI to the lockfile JSON to import.

  • strict – If True, remove products with hash mismatches.

Returns:

The imported (and possibly pruned) lockfile.

load(package: str, task: str, date: datetime, version: str | None = None) → DependencyLockFile | None[source]

Load an existing lockfile, or None.

Parameters:

  • package – Package name.

  • task – Task name.

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Optional package version.

property lockfile_dir: Path | CloudPath

The directory where aggregate lockfiles are stored.

static lockfile_name(package: str, task: str, date: datetime, version: str | None = None) → str[source]

Return the canonical lockfile filename for the given identity.

Parameters:

  • package – Package name.

  • task – Task name.

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Optional package version.

lockfile_path(package: str, task: str, date: datetime, version: str | None = None) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Return the expected path for a lockfile (may not exist yet).

Parameters:

  • package – Package name.

  • task – Task name.

  • date – Processing date.

  • version – Optional package version.

save(lockfile: DependencyLockFile) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Write (or overwrite) an aggregate lockfile.

Returns:

Path (local or cloud) to the written file.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.build_lock_product(sink: Path | CloudPath | str, url: str, name: str = '', description: str = '', alternative_urls: list[str] | None = None) → LockProduct[source]

Build a LockProduct from a local or cloud file.

Computes the SHA-256 hash and byte size of sink and packages them into a new LockProduct model.

Parameters:

  • sink – Path or URI to the file (local or cloud).

  • url – Primary remote URL the file was downloaded from.

  • name – Human-readable product name (e.g. 'ORBIT').

  • description – Free-text description of the product.

  • alternative_urls – Optional mirror / fallback URLs.

Returns:

A fully-populated LockProduct.

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If sink does not exist.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.get_dependency_lockfile(directory: Path | CloudPath | str, package: str, task: str, date: datetime | str, version: str | None = None) → tuple[DependencyLockFile | None, Path | CloudPath | None][source]

Read a DependencyLockFile from directory.

Parameters:

  • directory – Folder (local or cloud) containing lockfile JSON files.

  • package – Processing package name.

  • task – Processing task name.

  • date – Processing date used to derive the filename.

  • version – gnss-product-management package version. Defaults to the installed version.

Returns:

A (lockfile, path) tuple. If the file does not exist, lockfile is None but path is still returned so the caller knows where to write a new one.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.get_dependency_lockfile_name(package: str, task: str, date: str | datetime, version: str | None = None) → str[source]

Derive the canonical filename for a dependency lockfile.

The filename encodes package, task, year, day-of-year, and version so that each processing day gets its own file. Station is not part of the identity.

Parameters:

  • package – Processing package name (e.g. 'PRIDE').

  • task – Processing task name (e.g. 'PPP').

  • date – Processing date as a datetime or 'YYYY-MM-DD' string.

  • version – gnss-product-management package version. Defaults to the installed version.

Returns:

A filename string like PRIDE_PPP_2025_015_0.1.0_lock.json.

Raises:

ValueError – If date cannot be parsed.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.get_lock_product(sink: Path | CloudPath | str) → LockProduct | None[source]

Read the LockProduct sidecar JSON for sink.

Parameters:

sink – Path or URI to the downloaded product file.

Returns:

The deserialized LockProduct, or None if the sidecar file does not exist.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.get_lock_product_path(sink: Path | CloudPath | str) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Return the _lock.json sidecar path for a sink file.

Works for both local paths and cloud URIs — the sidecar is placed alongside the data file in the same storage backend.

Parameters:

sink – Path or URI to the downloaded product file.

Returns:

A path (local or cloud) pointing to the companion lock JSON (<sink>_lock.json).

Raises:

FileNotFoundError – If sink does not exist.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.get_package_version() → str[source]

Return the installed gnss-product-management version.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.validate_lock_product(product: LockProduct, mode: HashMismatchMode = HashMismatchMode.WARN) → bool[source]

Check that a lock-product’s sink file exists and its hash matches.

Also accepts the decompressed version of a .gz sink (e.g. foo.SP3 when the lock records foo.SP3.gz).

Parameters:

  • product – The lock-product entry to validate.

  • mode – How to handle hash mismatches. WARN logs a warning but returns True. STRICT returns False so the caller can re-download.

Returns:

True if the sink file (or its decompressed counterpart) exists and (when a hash is recorded) the file’s current SHA-256 matches the stored hash (or mode is WARN).

gnss_product_management.lockfile.write_dependency_lockfile(lockfile: DependencyLockFile, directory: Path | CloudPath | str, update: bool = False) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Write a DependencyLockFile to directory.

Parameters:

  • lockfile – The lockfile model to persist.

  • directory – Target directory (local path or cloud URI).

  • update – If True, overwrite an existing file. Otherwise raise FileExistsError.

Returns:

Path (local or cloud) to the written lockfile.

Raises:

FileExistsError – If the lockfile already exists and update is False.

gnss_product_management.lockfile.write_lock_product(lock_product: LockProduct) → Path | CloudPath[source]

Write a LockProduct to its sidecar _lock.json file.

Parameters:

lock_product – The lock entry to persist.

Returns:

Path (local or cloud) to the written sidecar file.
